disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The fall happened because she tripped over a chair that a visiting relative had moved, so her memorised layout of the room was wrong
trigger_question_examples: What happened just before you fell?
How did the fall happen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A relative visited and moved the furniture around. I stepped where I thought the floor was clear and there was a chair, and I went down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There is a long gaping cut across the front of the lower right leg that is bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Where are you hurt?
Can you describe the injury?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've torn open the front of my right shin. It's a long gash and it's bleeding. My spouse says it's gaping open.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The bleeding is a steady ooze rather than spurting, and it slows with firm direct pressure
trigger_question_examples: Is the bleeding heavy or spurting, or more of an ooze?
Does pressing on it slow the bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's oozing steadily, not squirting. When my spouse presses a towel on it firmly the bleeding slows right down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She is having an acute wave of panic with a pounding heart, fast breathing, trembling and sweating that came on with the fright of the fall
trigger_question_examples: How are you feeling in yourself right now?
Are you feeling panicky?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm panicking. My heart is pounding, I'm breathing really fast and shaking. I got such a fright and I can't calm myself down.

trigger_question_examples: Do you have any pain or tightness in your chest?
Any chest pain along with the racing heart?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no chest pain at all. It's just my heart racing from the fright.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: There was no dizziness, light-headedness or blacking out before the fall — it was a clear mechanical trip
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel faint or dizzy before you fell?
Did you black out at all, or did you just trip?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't feel dizzy or faint. I just caught my foot on the chair and fell — I was awake the whole time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She could put a little weight on the leg with help, and can move and feel her foot normally
trigger_question_examples: Can you move your foot and toes, and do they feel normal?
Were you able to put any weight on the leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I got a bit of weight on it with my spouse helping, though it hurt. My foot moves fine and feels normal, not numb.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She did not hit her head in the fall
trigger_question_examples: Did you hit your head when you went down?
Any bump to the head?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't hit my head. I came down on my side and my leg caught the chair.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She has been completely blind since birth with no light perception, and normally navigates independently with a guide dog
trigger_question_examples: Tell me a bit about your usual health and how you get around.
Do you have any long-term conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been blind since birth — I have no light perception at all. Normally I'm completely independent and get around with my guide dog.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Her tetanus immunisation is up to date and she takes no regular medicines
trigger_question_examples: Do you know if your tetanus shot is up to date?
Are you on any regular medications?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My tetanus is up to date, I checked recently. I don't take any regular medicines.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Her guide dog is shut in another room and her spouse is present and able to help
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone there with you right now?
Do you have someone who can help you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My spouse is right here with me. My guide dog is shut in another room at the moment.
